text: A double-lumen endotracheal tube was inserted for a right-sided uniportal VATS.
On exploration, there was bubbling identified from the posterior and basal mediastinum.
On closer inspection, three pleural peritoneal windows were identified, shown here.
The liver can be visualized directly through these defects.
Other operative findings included blood disease of the right upper lobe and right lower lobe, which were wedge resected using an endo-GIA purple stapler.
The diaphragmatic defects were closed using a number two stratific stitch with a clip to secure the end.
a 28 French curved chest tube was placed.
